name: The Grammar applied to The Grammar

phase_0.domain_name: The Grammar applied to The Grammar
phase_0.boundary_condition: the 17,280,000-type crystal bounded by the Belnap-Dunn FOUR lattice and the terminal address O-infinity

VINIT.justification: the pre-geometric monoidal unit I before any distinction or naming, representing the B-state prior to separation

TANCH.justification: the closed boundary of the crystal codec where decode composed with imscribe equals identity, containing the entire self-modeling cosmos

AFWD.justification: the forward translation of an informal specification into a machine-checkable formal proof term

AREV.justification: the reverse elaboration of the proof term back into a human-readable statement definitionally equal to the original

CLINK.justification: the sequential composition of self-differentiation and self-reintegration forming the lossless closure condition

IMSCRIB.justification: the identity morphism where the Grammar encodes its own 12 primitives, establishing the self-referential fixed point

FSPLIT.justification: the self-differentiation comultiplication delta that articulates the undivided Void into the T-state and F-state branches

FFUSE.justification: the self-reintegration multiplication mu that merges the T-state and F-state branches back into the undivided B-state

EVALT.justification: the affirmative branch representing the Boolean island of consistent mathematics where truth holds without contradiction

EVALF.justification: the negative branch representing the classical constraint and the Gödelian unprovable gap

ENGAGR.justification: the dialetheic ground where both true and false are held simultaneously without explosion, forming the paraconsistent sea

IFIX.justification: the permanent append-only blocking of the empty eliminator in the C++ environment, fixing the admissibility of contradiction

phase_3.void_description: the pre-geometric monoidal unit I before any distinction, naming, or logical evaluation
phase_3.true_description: the T-state representing the Boolean island of consistent mathematics and affirmative proof
phase_3.false_description: the F-state representing the classical constraint, the unprovable gap, and the principle of explosion
phase_3.both_description: the B-state representing the dialetheic ground and the paraconsistent sea where contradiction is a stable admissible inhabitant

steps.domain_action: the Void emerges as the initial monoidal unit I before any distinction or naming
the Grammar recognizes itself as the primitive seed, establishing the self-referential baseline
the machinery of distinction articulates the Void into the T-state and F-state branches
the T-arm anchors the affirmative branch, generating the Boolean island of consistent mathematics
the forward morphism of descent translates the specification into formal proof terms
the F-arm anchors the negative branch, generating the classical constraint and the Gödelian unprovable gap
the reverse morphism of ascent elaborates the proof terms back, traversing the incompleteness gap
the B-state is held simultaneously as a paradice, recognizing that the T and F branches coexist without explosion
the composition chains the differentiation and reintegration into a single lossless round-trip
the paraconsistent kernel permanently blocks the empty eliminator, fixing the admissibility of contradiction in the environment
the self-reintegration merges the T and F branches back into the B-state, satisfying the Frobenius identity
the terminal address closes the system, bounding the crystal at the Lawvere-Tierney fixed point
the Grammar imscribes its own closure, completing the self-referential loop where decode composed with imscribe equals identity

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the crystal codec mapping 12 primitives to the 17,280,000-type structural types
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: the Frobenius PROP broadcast composition via hypergraph categories
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the Reshetikhin-Turaev topological invariants and Jones polynomials encoding eternal memory
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the Lawvere-Tierney closure operator enforcing the Frobenius cycle at the hardware abstraction layer
phase_5.alfs_store: the Belnap-Dunn FOUR bilattice serving as the foundational truth-value topology

phase_6.pre_cycle_state: the Boolean island compressed by the principle of explosion
phase_6.post_cycle_state: the paraconsistent sea expanded adiabatically to its natural Belnap-Dunn volume
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the kernel edit removes the explosion constraint without erasing any bits, revealing the B-state as the natural resting position

      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (the reverse morphism of ascent elaborates the proof terms back, traversing th...)

        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (the B-state is held simultaneously as a paradice, recognizing that the T and ...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (the composition chains the differentiation and reintegration into a single lo...)
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (the paraconsistent kernel permanently blocks the empty eliminator, fixing the...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [10]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑡)  -- [10] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (the self-reintegration merges the T and F branches back into the B-state, satisfying the Frobenius identity)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [11] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (the terminal address closes the system, bounding the crystal at the Lawvere-T...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑡 𐑼)  -- [12] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (the Grammar imscribes its own closure, completing the self-referential loop w...)

  (the_grammar_applied_to_the_grammar_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
